title: dortmanna linearis
intro: Dortmanna linearis, commonly known as the Narrow-leaved Dortmannia or Narrow-leaved Dortmanna, is a type of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of various conditions. The therapeutic actions of Dortmanna linearis are believed to be attributed to its ability to relax muscles, reduce fever, and act as an antispasmodic agent. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Dortmanna linearis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high alkaloid content. Overconsumption can also lead to cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations, in sensitive individuals. In some cases, Dortmanna linearis may cause allergic reactions, manifesting as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Moreover, excessive use can disrupt liver function and potentially lead to liver damage if not properly monitored.
